subject: Re: disk1.cab error with Warp_1.0.9 install
body:
Thanks Alister! I did come across it! I ended up finding a solution between 
that post and others, in case anyone comes across the same problem...

I switched from Edge -> Brave for the .msi download. I downloaded the .msi 
file to the "Desktop" directory instead of "Downloads" directory. Then ran 
the installer from "Desktop". Curiously that worked. Hope others find that 
helpful!

On Tuesday, August 2, 2022 at 1:20:10 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Jacob,
>
> Some tips here, did you come across this? 
> https://answers.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/forum/all/why-is-disk1cab-corrupt-on-every-software-download/83ec4491-52b7-4252-9619-0e487fd6d5e9
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On 2 Aug 2022, at 20:49, Jacob Anderson <jacro...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Fresh Windows 10 install today with the goal of using the workstation for 
> Warp/M. After  assuring the pre-requisites were in order (.Net Framework 
> 4.8 downloaded, Visual C++ 2017 Redistributable installed + restarted, + 
> newest NVIDIA drivers for a 3090 RTX), I downloaded Warp_1.0.9. I then ran 
> the installer. 
>
>
> I get the error "*The cabinent file 'disk1.cab' required for this 
> installation is corrupt and cannot be used. This could indicate a network 
> error, an error reading from the CD-ROM, or a problem with this package*."
>
> On clicking "Retry" the error recurs. On clicking ignore I then get a new 
> error: "FDI server error".
>
> I performed an iteration of deleting the installer and redownloading and 
> running the installer. This reproduced the issue. 
>
> I am curious if anyone has encountered and overcome this problem on the 
> install. Or if this installer error is diagnostic of another problem.
>
> [image: Screenshot (4).png]
>
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups

> .

>
